title: How Do I Know If My Website Design Is Good?
excerpt: Many business owners aren't sure how to objectively evaluate their own website. This post gives you clear, measurable criteria — from design quality to analytics data — to determine whether your website is an asset or a liability for your business.
content:
It's hard to be objective about your own website. You know your business too well — you fill in gaps that visitors don't, and you've looked at the same pages so many times they've lost all meaning. So how do you get an honest read on whether your website design is actually good?

Here are the indicators to look for.

Quantitative Signals (Check Your Analytics)

Bounce Rate: If the majority of visitors leave after viewing a single page, that's a signal something isn't engaging them. Industry averages vary, but a bounce rate above 70–80% is worth investigating. Average Session Duration: Are visitors spending meaningful time on your site? Very short sessions suggest the content isn't relevant or the design isn't holding attention. Conversion Rate: Are visitors taking action — calling, enquiring, signing up, purchasing? If you're getting traffic but no conversions, your design may be failing to guide and persuade. Mobile Traffic Performance: Are mobile visitors converting at a similar rate to desktop users? A large gap suggests your mobile experience needs work.

Qualitative Signals (Ask Real People)

  • Ask someone who doesn't know your business to spend 30 seconds on your homepage and tell you what your company does
  • Use a tool like Hotjar or Microsoft Clarity to watch real user session recordings
  • Ask for honest feedback from recent customers about their website experience

Design Quality Signals

  • Does your website look current, or does it feel dated?
  • Is there a clear visual hierarchy on every page?
  • Is text easy to read on mobile?
  • Are your CTAs clear and prominent?
  • Does your site load in under 3 seconds?

The Fastest Way to Know

Show your site to an experienced web designer or digital strategist and ask for an honest critique. A good professional will tell you exactly what's working and what needs to change.
